item.title: RMG unit reopening, payment demanded
item.description: Leaders of National Garment Workers Federation on Thursday at a rally in Dhaka called on Bangladesh Nationalist Party government to take steps...

item.title: AAUB holds 19th academic council meeting
item.description: The 19th academic council meeting of Aviation and Aerospace University, Bangladesh was held at the university’s conference room on Dhaka campus on Thursday....

item.title: Wage, allowance demanded for restaurant workers before eid
item.description: Labour leaders on Thursday at a protest rally called on the owners of hotels and restaurants to pay the wages and festival allowances of the workers as per the announced wage board....

item.title: New publishers promote new writers
item.description: At the Amar Ekushey Book Fair 2026, a total of 34 new publishing houses are participating, and they mostly promote new writers....

item.title: Jamaat blames Rizwana, Khalilur for ‘election engineering’
item.description: The Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Thursday demanded that former interim government adviser Syeda Rizwana Hasan and former security adviser Khalilur Rahman, now serving as foreign minister, be questioned and tried over allegations of ‘election engineering’....

item.title: 207 traffic congestion-prone spots identified on different highways
item.description: The authorities concerned have identified 207 traffic congestion-prone spots on different highways across the country before the forthcoming Eid-ul-Fitr, one of the biggest religious festivals of the Muslims....

item.title: BNP, Jamaat neither confirm nor deny involvement in US trade deal
item.description: Leaders of two major political parties — the Bangladesh Nationalist Party and the Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami — have neither endorsed nor explicitly rejected the foreign minister Khalilur Rahman’s assertion that they were kept in the loop on a trade agreement with......

item.title: Push-in anxiety and BNP’s test of statecraft
item.description: IN BANGLADESH, borders are never merely cartographic lines. They are political weather systems. When the climate shifts in New Delhi or Naypyidaw, Dhaka feels the wind first along its riverbanks and barbed wire. Today, as controversy swirls around the large-scale exclusion and adjudication of millions of names in West Bengal’s voter revision process, the conversation......

item.title: Nepal awaits results in key post-uprising polls
item.description: Nepal voted on Thursday for a new parliament in a high-stakes showdown between an entrenched old guard and a powerful youth movement, six months after deadly anti-corruption protests toppled the government....

item.title: A mandate in the plains, but dissent in the hills
item.description: THE recent referendum, endorsed by 68 per cent of voters nationwide, has been celebrated as a decisive mandate to steer Bangladesh back toward democratic reform after years of authoritarian drift. But in the Chittagong Hill Tracts, the verdict was starkly different. Indigenous communities in Rangamati, Khagrachari, and Bandarban rejected the reform package by......

item.title: South Point’s 115 students selected for scholarship
item.description: One hundred and fifteen students from South Point School and College have been selected for scholarships in the recently published results of the junior scholarship examination 2025 under the Dhaka Board of Intermediate and Secondary Education....

item.title: Yunus government failed, not July uprising
item.description: THE fall of Sheikh Hasina’s regime on 5 August 2024 marked the end of one of the most prolonged periods of authoritarian hold in Bangladesh’s history. For a decade, since the 2014 election and the successively rigged polls of 2018 and 2024, Hasina governed by suppressing dissent, distorting democratic institutions, and crushing any space for genuine political......

item.title: High toll of remittances
item.description: OVERSEAS migration outflow and remittance inflow has for long been powering the economic engine, supporting household income of millions and strengthening foreign exchange reserves that drives the overall development......

item.title: Sudden shake-up raises questions
item.description: A difficult political transition has followed the parliamentary elections of February 12, ushering in a significant new chapter in the country’s governance. The Bangladesh Nationalist Party, which secured an absolute majority and assumed office on February 17, has moved swiftly to reshape key democratic institutions. Within weeks, the governor of Bangladesh Bank was......

item.title: 34 more ME flights cancelled, 210 in 6 days
item.description: At least 34 more Middle Eastern flights were cancelled to and from Hazrat Shahjalal International Airport in Dhaka city on Thursday, raising the total number of flights cancelled to 210 in the past six days due to the conflict involving the Gulf region, a major......

item.title: Welcome move that needs careful planning
item.description: THE government’s plan to introduce women-only buses in Dhaka is a welcome move as it can address the persistent troubling reality of urban life for tens of thousands of women. For the women who rely on public transport, the commute is often accompanied by anxiety about harassment ranging from offensive remarks to physical intimidation. A dedicated bus service for......

item.title: Portfolio change questions earlier judgement
item.description: THE prime minister, Tarique Rahman, has redistributed the portfolios of eight ministers of state and added to responsibilities of two advisers to the prime minister. The portfolios of three have been reduced to one from three; the portfolios of three have been reduced to one from two; and, the portfolios of two ministers of state have been reduced to one from three while......

item.title: Litton, Afif back for Pakistan ODIs
item.description: Litton Das and Afif Hossain were recalled as the Bangladesh Cricket Board on Thursday announced the squad for the three-match ODI series against Pakistan starting on March 11......

item.title: Kiwis’ confidence high for final
item.description: Semi-final hero Finn Allen said New Zealand’s thumping demi-final win over South Africa was a ‘huge confidence booster’ ahead of going for their maiden T20 World Cup title in Sunday’s final......

item.title: Bangladesh aim to win hearts against DPRK
item.description: Bangladesh will be looking to win hearts when they face three-time champions North Korea in their AFC Women’s Asian Cup Group B second match at the Western Sydney Stadium in Australia today......

item.title: No choke but ‘walloping’, SA coach after flop
item.description: South Africa coach Shukri Conrad on Wednesday made no excuses and called his team’s nine-wicket defeat by New Zealand in the semi-finals of the T20 World Cup a ‘walloping’......

item.title: Fatherhood, sleep, final: Henry’s whirlwind journey
item.description: New Zealand’s Matt Henry played a doting father to his new-born son back home and returned to help his team reach the T20 World Cup final, all in a space of five days......

item.title: BGB seizes smuggled goods worth Tk 131cr in February
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh seized various types of smuggled goods worth over Tk 131.19 crore during its drives in the country’s border and other areas in February....

item.title: JnU Journalists’ Association polls postponed as clashes erupt, 10 injured
item.description: The election commission of Jagannath University Journalists’ Association on Thursday postponed the March 8 polls as clashes were erupted centring on the nomination submission and withdrawal of the polls at the university in the Old Dhaka on Thursday....

item.title: Minister vows action against doctors’ private practice during duty
item.description: Health and family welfare minister Sardar Md Sakhawat Hossain warned that undisciplined doctors might face strict disciplinary action for any negligence to duties and private practice in during office hour....

item.title: 3 auto-rickshaw drivers held in Sunamganj for raping teenage girl
item.description: The police arrested three drivers of CNG-run auto-rickshaws in Sunamganj on Thursday on charge of raping a teenage girl....

item.title: Court attaches Motijheel Ideal School official Atiqur’s assets
item.description: A Dhaka court on Thursday ordered the attachment of some immovable properties of Motijheel Ideal School and College’s deputy assistant engineer Atiqur Rahman Khan, over graft allegations....

item.title: IU students rally for justice for slain teacher
item.description: STUDENTS of Islamic University staged a demonstration on the campus in Kushtia on Thursday demanding justice for the murder of the university’s social welfare department chairperson assistant professor Asma Sadia Runa....

item.title: India reach T20 World Cup final after England fail in epic chase
item.description: India clung on to beat England by seven runs in a thrilling T20 World Cup semi-final in Mumbai on Thursday, despite Jacob Bethell's superb 105 that put Harry Brook's side within a whisker of pulling off a record chase....

item.title: ICT adjourns hearing till March 30
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Thursday adjourned till March 30 the hearing of a case filed against 13 people, including ousted prime minister Sheikh Hasina, over enforced disappearances and torture at the Joint Interrogation Cell......

item.title: Action taken against Shiddhirganj land office irregularities
item.description: Punitive measures have been taken against those responsible for irregularities found during an inspection of the Siddhirganj union land office in Narayanganj....

item.title: PM for launching language training centre for overseas jobs
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman on Thursday directed the authorities concerned to establish language training centres across the country to develop skilled manpower for overseas employment....

item.title: Ukraine, Russia free 200 POWs each
item.description: Ukraine and Russia freed 200 prisoners of war each on Thursday, the first part of an exchange that will see them swap 500 people each in total, officials said....

item.title: India condoles Khamenei’s death
item.description: India formally condoled the death of Iran’s supreme leader Ayatullah Ali Khamenei on Thursday....

item.title: Wary European states pledge military aid for Cyprus, Gulf
item.description: Wary of getting directly involved in the US-Israeli war with Iran, European countries are nevertheless being drawn into the conflict following attacks on Cyprus and Western allies in the Gulf....

item.title: Explain steps taken to recover looted arms: HC
item.description: The High Court on Thursday issued a rule asking the government to explain in four weeks what steps it had taken to recover a large number of firearms and ammunition looted during the July 2024 mass uprising....

item.title: Cable trader found dead in Dhaka
item.description: A 28-year-old cable trader was found dead at a house in Bhatara area of Dhaka early Thursday....

item.title: EC validates 3 nomination papers in Bogura by-election
item.description: The election commission on Thursday declared the nomination papers of three candidates, including those from Bangladesh Nationalist Party and Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami, valid for the Bogura-6, Sadar, parliamentary by-election scheduled for April 9....

item.title: Govt to start canal excavation programme from Dinajpur
item.description: The government is set to launch a nationwide canal excavation and re-excavation programme in Dinajpur before Eid-ul-Fitr, one of the biggest religious festivals of the Muslims, to be celebrated in the country on March 20 or 21 subject to the sighting of moon....
